Splunk Data Source Assurance Model

Platform Taxonomy with Logging Patterns and Expanded Health Checks

Taxonomy concepts are arranged beneath their logical parents. Populated workbook records appear under Current entries, while the governed naming standard is available under Governance & Assurance → Platform Governance Profile. Use the advisor to select from 39 destination-specific patterns, including cloud-supported apps, on-premises add-ons, Splunk Stream, Heavy Forwarder security-zone intermediary designs and syslog-server forwarder collection, with pattern-specific firewall, authentication and source-platform prerequisites. The SIEM Data Source Catalogue adds 18 governed source families and coverage mappings. The selectable Health Checks Table contains 390 reusable assurance templates, including dedicated Heavy Forwarder platform/intermediary, syslog-server file collection, Azure Function runtime and AWS Lambda runtime controls alongside the existing event-quality, multi-host, Ingest Processor, Splunk Stream, REST/script, Universal Forwarder, osquery, Sysmon and raw TCP checks.

61 taxonomy terms 716 current entries 16 populated sources 18 reference source families 39 governed logging patterns 390 reusable health checks Entry groups are collapsed by default
Lifecycle & Dependency · TAX-06.04

Data Source Assurance

Operational assurance view centred on configured input components. It joins collection configuration, current test results, CIM compliance, detection coverage, dependencies and lifecycle evidence without duplicating the underlying taxonomy records. Select a source to review its end-to-end assurance profile or open the source directly in the taxonomy explorer.

Operational sources

Input components are the primary operational assurance record until a dedicated Data Sources register is populated.

0 visible
ComponentData sourceInput typePlatformOwnerHealthCIMDetectionAssurance

Onboarding pipeline

Lifecycle records remain separate from deployed components until a stable component or data-source identifier is assigned.

0 visible
RequestData sourceRequestorBusiness ownerInput typePlatformIndexSourcetypeStageTACIMHealth testsUATTarget date
Data Collection & Routing · TAX-03.01

SIEM Data Source Catalogue

Reference catalogue of the security and operational source families the SIEM should cover. Catalogue records describe intended telemetry and use-case value; explicit mappings connect them to configured input components, current assurance results and onboarding work without treating reference requirements as deployed inputs.

Reference source families

Coverage is derived from approved stable-ID mappings to operational components, onboarding records and registered platforms.

0 visible
SourceCategorySub-categoryPriorityCoverageImplementationsOnboardingAssurance
Data Collection & Routing · TAX-03.02

Logging Patterns Table

Compare all 39 governed logging patterns, including conditional port/firewall, authentication and source-platform prerequisites. Filter by destination or category, search across every field, export the visible records, or select a Pattern ID to open its full taxonomy detail. Ports shown are common defaults and must be validated against the approved local design.

39 visible patterns 20 Splunk Cloud 19 Splunk on-premises 13 comparison columns

Events & Data Quality · TAX-04.04

Health Checks Table

Review all 390 reusable health-check templates, including 16 Heavy Forwarder platform/intermediary checks, 14 syslog-server file collection checks, 12 Azure Function runtime checks and 12 AWS Lambda runtime checks alongside the existing event-quality, multi-host, Ingest Processor, Splunk Stream, REST/script, Universal Forwarder, osquery, Sysmon and raw TCP checks. Search across every field, filter by input type, category or priority, export the visible records, or select a Test ID to open its complete taxonomy detail.

Data Collection & Routing · TAX-03.02

Logging Pattern Advisor

Answer the source, supportability, runtime, destination and network-boundary questions to receive a governed destination-specific pattern, architecture flow, implementation controls and health profile.

Collection questions

Choose the closest answer. The advisor evaluates all 39 active variants, including direct HEC, Heavy Forwarder HEC, scripted REST API polling and syslog-server file collection through a Splunk forwarder.

Used for LP-017 when no supported add-on exists and the source cannot push to HEC.
Ready to assessComplete the questions and select Suggest pattern.

Governance & Assurance · TAX-02.09

Naming Convention

A single standard for taxonomy registry IDs, Splunk object names, event-health checks, detection content and analytical assets.

Technical-name pattern

Use this structure where a technical object needs platform, dataset, purpose and environment context.

<domain>_ <platform>_ <component>_ <purpose>_ <environment>
Examplesec_azure_entra_signin_prodValid structure
Grandfathering: existing production indexes and sourcetypes do not need to be renamed solely for style. Record exceptions and apply the standard to new or materially changed objects.

Name validator

Check a proposed name against the selected governed pattern.

Pattern catalogue

Registry identifiers align with the current taxonomy and workbook records; technical patterns define how new Splunk content should be named.

ObjectPatternExampleSeparator / caseScope
Platform IDPLT-###PLT-003Hyphen / uppercaseRegistry
Source Family IDSRC-###SRC-001Hyphen / uppercaseReference catalogue
Governance Profile IDGOV-###GOV-003Hyphen / uppercaseRegistry
Logging Pattern IDLP-###LP-006Hyphen / uppercaseRegistry
Input Component IDIC-###IC-011Hyphen / uppercaseRegistry
Collector IDCOL-###COL-005Hyphen / uppercaseRegistry
App / TA IDAPP-###APP-003Hyphen / uppercaseRegistry
Stanza IDSTZ-###STZ-021Hyphen / uppercaseRegistry
Health Test IDTC-<TYPE>-###TC-HEC-004Hyphen / uppercaseAssurance
Dependency IDDEP-###DEP-007Hyphen / uppercaseLineage
Onboarding IDONB-###ONB-005Hyphen / uppercaseWorkflow
Index<domain_or_platform>[_<dataset>]aws_cloudtrailUnderscore / lowercaseSplunk
Sourcetype<vendor>:<product>:<dataset>azure:aad:signinColon / lowercase preferredSplunk
Event Health CheckHC_<platform>_<source>_<check>HC_entra_signin_freshnessUnderscore / lowercaseAssurance
Detection Use Case IDDET-<DOMAIN>-###DET-IDM-012Hyphen / uppercaseDetection
Detection Search<Domain> - <Platform> - <Behaviour>Identity - Entra ID - Repeated MFA DenialTitle case / spaced hyphenDetection
Dashboard<Domain> - <Platform> - <Audience>Security - Entra ID - OperationsTitle case / spaced hyphenAnalytics

Governance rules

01Stable identifiers

Never reuse an ID after retirement. Display names can change; registry IDs must remain stable.

02Controlled separators

Use hyphens for registry IDs, underscores for technical names and colons for sourcetype hierarchy.

03No embedded secrets

Never include tokens, credentials, personal data or sensitive business values in object names.

04Environment last

When environment is required, use a controlled suffix such as dev, test, uat or prod.

Use

  • TC-AWS-002
  • HC_entra_signin_freshness
  • azure:aad:signin
  • Identity - Entra ID - Repeated MFA Denial

Avoid

  • new_test_2
  • AzureLogs
  • hec-token-prod-9f2…
  • Critical Alert
